text: The patient is positioned in a Trendelenburg position with the left side up. The DaVinci XI robot is brought in, aligning the crosshairs with the camera port.

The surgeon aligns the trajectory of the camera port with the center of the dissection, arranging the flex joints parallel to the camera port arm. The top-down docking approach begins with pointing the laser at the camera port, followed by visual alignment of the flex joints once the robot is docked.

The camera shows the patient clearance joints of the robotic system moving downward. These joints are adjusted to descend as far as possible without making contact with the patient, applicable across various surgical procedures.
The surgeon clears the nozzles of the robotic ports to prevent contact with the patient during the operation, completing the manual docking process of the XI robot.
